body:
Hi Chloe,

Hope you're doing well. Quick question — been meaning to ask you about the drip coffee situation at home. We're going through beans faster than I'd like, and I want to lock in a reliable order before I run out mid-week. Nothing worse than starting a 12-hour shift without two decent cups in me.

Right now I'm grinding whole bean and brewing in the Chemex, but I'm open to a subscription if the quality holds. Looking for something dark, low acidity. Maybe a medium-dark roast. Budget isn't a concern as long as it's consistent.

Can you pull up some options? Preferably something that ships to Stamford within two days. Need it sorted before Thursday.

Thanks,
Nate

Hi Nate,

Got it — dark, low acidity, medium-dark roast, ships to Stamford by Thursday. I've pulled three options that fit.

First is Trade Coffee's subscription — they let you dial in roast level and grind preference, and two-day shipping is standard. Second is Onyx Coffee Lab's Southern Weather blend, ships in one to two days, consistently rated low acidity and works well for pour-over. Third is Intelligentsia's Black Cat Classic Espresso, which a lot of Chemex users actually prefer for its body and clean finish.

All three are available for one-time purchase if you want to try before committing to a subscription. I'd suggest starting with the Onyx or Intelligentsia given your setup and timeline.

Want me to go ahead and place an order, or would you like to review first?

Best,
Chloe
Customer Support

Hi,

Reaching out about order #7841 placed eleven days ago — Vitamin D3 2000 IU and fish oil 1000 mg, monthly supply. Carrier portal has said "in transit" since the 19th. Nothing at the door.

I'm on 12-hour ED shifts most of the week and the supplements are part of the morning routine before I walk out of the house at six-forty-five. Running low on the D3 now. Can you check whether the shipment is actually moving or whether I need a replacement sent?

Thanks,
Nate Wright

Hi Dad — pulling up the order now.

Carrier flagged a delay out of the regional sort facility. Estimated delivery got pushed to this Friday. I've gone ahead and added a one-time courtesy refill to ship today with expedited, so you won't be running a deficit on the D3 before then. Should arrive Wednesday.

Quick confirm before it goes out — original order was the fish oil 1000 mg softgels, 60 count. Still the right quantity? Want to make sure I'm not sending you sixty capsules when you're used to ninety.

— Chloe
Customer Support, VitaSource

Chloe,

Wednesday works. 60 count is correct on the fish oil — that's what I've been taking.

Good catch. Appreciate the expedite.

Tell your mother I said hi. She mentioned you might be coming up this weekend. We'll see what the schedule looks like.

Nate

Chloe,

Just got home. Twelve hours at Harborview will do that. Noticed you didn't respond in the group chat last night — Mom and Nolan were both on, I checked before I crashed. Not an accusation, just flagging it.

We keep that chat going for a reason. Works better when all three of you are actually in it.

Let me know you're good.

— Dad

Dad,

Sorry — I saw it late and figured everyone was asleep. Didn't want to ping at 11pm. I'm good, just had a study session run long.

For the record, I did read everything. Mom sent three paragraphs about the neighbor's dog and Nolan sent a meme I still don't fully understand. You were clearly already out by then.

I'll be better about same-day responses. Promise.

— Chloe

Chloe,

A late thumbs-up still counts. Doesn't have to be a paragraph.

I get the long nights — you're not the only one pulling them. But your mother notices when read receipts don't come back. That's the part that matters, not the response time.

The meme was fine. Nolan walked me through it this morning over coffee. Took two cups.

— Dad

Dad,

Noted on the thumbs-up. I didn't realize Mom was actively tracking read receipts — that's a level of group chat oversight I should have anticipated years ago.

I'll check in earlier going forward. And I'll let Nolan know his meme required a full debrief and a second cup of coffee. He'll be insufferable about it.

Hope tomorrow's shift is quiet.

— Chloe

Chloe,

Just got off. Shift ended at 19:00, which means I have maybe two hours before my brain stops working entirely. Three traumas before 10:00, and Phil Engstrom decided the second one was a good time to walk a student through thoracic anatomy. Respect the commitment. Less respect for the timing.

Reason I'm writing — the shift summary your team sent over has a coverage gap flagged on the 15th that doesn't line up with what Harborview has on file. The 0700 start is confirmed on my end. The gap isn't. I need that corrected in writing before the week closes, otherwise scheduling will use it to justify a call-in I don't have capacity for.

Can you flag it on your end and get a corrected confirmation out? Email works. No calls tonight.

— Dad

Dad,

Already on it. Pulled the original submission and cross-referenced against Harborview's calendar — the 15th discrepancy was a data entry error on our side. Corrected confirmation will be in your inbox by 0800 tomorrow, well before your first cup.

Also, Dr. Engstrom mid-trauma anatomy lecture is somehow both the most and least surprising thing you've told me this week.

Saw the family chat was quiet tonight — figured you were in recovery mode. Mom mentioned you skipped the group check-in, which means you were either asleep by 20:30 or just staring at the ceiling. Either's valid after a shift like that.

Get some rest. Confirmation comes tomorrow morning. Let me know if anything else needs sorting.

— Chloe

Chloe,

Following up on the Gmail API ticket from last week. I set up the OAuth credentials the way your colleague walked me through, but the authorization flow still errors out at the redirect step. Getting a 400 redirect_uri_mismatch and I've checked the URI three times against what's in the console.

I need this working because the family group chat idea I had — pulling message summaries for the week so I don't miss anything from you, Nolan, or your mom when I'm mid-shift — depends on it. Twelve hours in the ED and I surface to fourteen unread messages. Not sustainable.

Let me know what I'm missing.

Dad

Hi Dad,

The 400 redirect_uri_mismatch usually means the URI registered in Google Cloud Console doesn't exactly match what your app is sending — including trailing slashes and http vs https. Common miss.

Can you paste the exact redirect URI you have in the console and the one in your code? I'll compare them. Also, make sure the OAuth client type is set to 'Web application' and not 'Desktop app' if you're doing a browser-based flow. Those have different redirect requirements.

Once I see both strings I can probably turn this around fast. And yes, please get the group chat thing working — Mom has been sending voice memos and you are the only one not reacting to them.

Chloe
Support — Google Workspace Integrations

Chloe,

Found it. Trailing slash. Console had it, code didn't. Classic.

Flow works now. I'll wire up the summary logic this weekend if I'm not wrecked after Saturday's shift. Authorization tokens are caching correctly and scopes look right.

Tell your team the documentation on redirect URI formatting could use one extra sentence. Would have saved me four days.

And I heard the voice memos. I reacted. Heart emoji counts.

Dad

Hi Chloe,

Following up on my ticket from last Thursday. The Google Calendar API integration isn't syncing correctly — events created before June 15 aren't populating in the shared view. My 12-hour ED shifts at Harborview show up fine when I enter them manually, but the retroactive pull is broken. My colleague Dr. Engstrom is seeing the same thing on his end.

I work 7 to 7, so I'm unreachable most of the day on shift days. After 7 PM I'm usually offline until morning. Best to reach me by email.

Good name, by the way.

Nate Wright
PA-C, Emergency Department
Harborview Regional Medical Center

Hi Nate,

Thanks for following up — and I hear that about the name more than you'd think.

We've confirmed a known issue with the Calendar API's incremental sync endpoint affecting events created before June 15. A token refresh bug in our latest update caused the gap. Engineering pushed a patch this morning; a full resync should complete within 24 hours for most accounts.

In the meantime, you can force a manual resync from Settings > Integrations > Google Calendar > Resync Now. Usually resolves in under five minutes. Can you confirm you and your colleague are both on the same workspace tier? That'll help us verify you're both covered under the fix.

Chloe Wright
Support Team

Chloe,

Both on Professional tier. Ran the manual resync. My own pre-June-15 events are back. Dr. Engstrom's calendar is still showing gaps — specifically his recurring blocks, not one-off entries.

Tried resync twice on his end. Same result.

We both have shifts entered as repeating events, Monday Wednesday Friday through August. Not sure if the recurring event logic gets handled differently in the sync pipeline. Worth flagging to engineering if it isn't already on their radar.

On shift tomorrow, 7 to 7. Won't be checking email until evening.

Nate

Hi Nate,

Good catch on the recurring events — that's a separate issue from the one the patch addressed. Repeating events with an end date beyond July 31 weren't covered in this fix. Engineering has it logged and the targeted deploy is tomorrow morning.

Fastest workaround for Dr. Engstrom in the meantime: delete and re-enter one recurring series manually. That forces a fresh token for those events and the rest of the series should populate correctly within a few minutes.

I'll send a follow-up once the patch deploys to confirm his calendar is restored. Nothing else needed from either of you tonight — go decompress.

Chloe Wright
Support Team

Hi,

Following up on ticket #4821. Submitted the WhatsApp Business API access request last Tuesday and the status has been sitting at 'under review' since. I need the webhook endpoint live before July 7 — we're piloting a patient callback notification system at Harborview and the go-live window is tight.

I'm on shift 7 to 7 most days, so email is the better channel. Phone calls during the day are a non-starter. Can someone confirm the review timeline and let me know if there's anything pending on my end?

Thanks,
Nate Wright
PA-C, Emergency Department
Harborview Regional Medical Center

Hi Nate,

Thanks for the follow-up. Ticket #4821 is sitting in the final verification queue right now. Standard turnaround from submission is 3 to 5 business days, which puts you at Thursday or Friday this week barring anything unusual.

Once it clears, the webhook endpoint and API credentials will go to the email address on file. Given the healthcare context, I can flag it to our compliance team for an expedited review — just reply here with a confirm and I'll escalate it today.

Also — Dad. You have my number. You did not need to file a formal support ticket to reach me. The dry humor in 'phone calls during the day are a non-starter' was noted, but still.

Chloe Wright
Support Team, API Onboarding

Chloe,

Following up on ticket SR-4471 from last week. The third-party playlist sync app I set up for the ED break room is still throwing a 403 on every OAuth token refresh. Initial auth works fine. Breaks on every refresh cycle after that.

You mentioned the dev team was investigating scope changes on the Spotify API side. Any update? The residents have decided a working playlist is essential to the functioning of the department. I have no opinion on this.

On a 7-to-7 today. Will check back tonight.

— Dad

Dad,

Good news — the team pushed a fix to the OAuth refresh endpoint yesterday evening. Root cause was a scope mismatch introduced in the 3.2 API update. The user-read-playback-state permission wasn't being re-granted on refresh cycles, which is what was triggering the 403s.

Fix on your end is straightforward: go into your Spotify account settings, revoke the app's authorization entirely, then re-authenticate from scratch. That forces a clean token issuance with the corrected scopes attached. If it still errors after that, reply with the new error code and I'll escalate.

You can tell the residents their infrastructure is restored. Take full credit. You earned it for filing a coherent bug report, which is rarer than you'd think.

Talk soon,
Chloe

Chloe,

Just got home. Re-auth worked. Playlists loading, no errors.

Told them it was fixed. Did not mention you. Credibility in that department is a limited resource and I am conserving it.

Appreciate you pushing on it.

— Dad

Hi,

Order #48821 arrived Thursday — dark roast, selected medium-fine grind at checkout. What showed up is coarse. French press territory. I brew black drip, nothing added, two to three cups before 7 AM on shift days. That grind doesn't extract right and I don't have time to troubleshoot it at 5:45 in the morning.

Need a replacement bag sent out. Same roast, correct grind. What's the turnaround?

Nate Wright

Hi Nate,

Replacement is already queued — ships tomorrow, arrives by Wednesday. That grind batch was miscalibrated on our end; you weren't the first to flag it this week. We're pulling it from inventory.

Also — hi. It's Chloe. Of every ticket I get assigned on a Monday morning, I did not expect this one.

Chloe Wright
Customer Support

Chloe,

Wednesday works.

While I have you on record: my subscription is set to ship every 30 days. I go through two bags in that window, sometimes three if the week runs heavy with 12-hour shifts. I'd rather not run out mid-week. Can you change the interval to every 14 days, two bags per shipment? Same roast, same grind — assuming you all have the calibration sorted.

Also tell your grandmother I said hello. She texted the family group chat at 11 PM again.

— Nate (Order #48821)

Hi Dad,

Done. Subscription updated to every 14 days, two bags per shipment, Harborview Dark Roast, medium-fine grind. First adjusted delivery goes out the 12th — should land well before your next run of pre-dawn shifts.

Grandma texted at 11 PM because no one replied to her 9 PM message. Chain of events.

Ticket closed. You can tell Mom I was helpful.

Chloe Wright
Customer Support

Hi,

Following up on a question I submitted last week about my current supplement stack. I've been taking Vitamin D3 2000 IU and fish oil 1000 mg every morning — both from your brand — for about four months. Started them alongside my first coffee before the drive to Harborview.

Past two weeks I've noticed the fish oil softgels have a stronger smell than the previous batch. Not rancid exactly, but off. Checked the lot number and it's different from what I had before. Wondering if there was a formulation or supplier change.

Also want to confirm the D3 I'm getting is D3 and not D2. Label says cholecalciferol so I assume we're fine, but figured I'd ask while I had you.

Nate Wright

Hi Nate,

Thanks for reaching out and for the detail — it actually helps a lot.

The smell difference is real. We switched to a new cold-pressed fish oil source in the current production run. The oil itself tests clean — peroxide and TOTOX values are within spec — but the sensory profile is noticeably fishier than the previous supplier. We're aware of it and it's on the radar for the next batch. If it's bothering you, refrigerating the softgels before you take them usually cuts the smell significantly.

And yes — the D3 is cholecalciferol throughout. No D2 in that product line.

Let me know if you want a replacement bottle sent out while we work through this lot. Happy to sort that.

Chloe Wright
Customer Support, Harborview Wellness Supply

Chloe —

Checked the chat after shift. You mentioned maybe coming up to Stamford sometime in July — I want to lock that down before your mother turns it into a four-week planning committee.

Nolan said he could drive up from Providence if you're there the 12th or 13th. That works on my end too. I'm on a 7-to-7 at Harborview the 11th, off the 12th. The 13th I'm back on, so Saturday is the day.

Send me actual dates when you have them.

— Dad

Dad —

July 12th works. I'll drive up Friday evening and leave Sunday morning — gives us a full Saturday at least.

Can you ask Mom to not over-schedule it? Last visit she had an actual printed itinerary. I just want to sit on the porch with coffee and exist for a day. Also tell Nolan to confirm for real this time. I'm not driving four hours to eat dinner alone with you two.

Also I saw your coffee order in the chat. Three cups before 9 AM is not a personality, it's a warning sign.

— Chloe

Chloe —

July 12th is on the calendar. Told your mother. She has already asked about the dinner menu, so I can't make promises about the itinerary, but I'll try to keep it under one laminated page.

Nolan confirmed. He used two words and a thumbs-up emoji, which I'm choosing to interpret as enthusiasm.

The three cups are non-negotiable. Drive safe on the 12th. Text when you're an hour out.

— Dad

Chloe,

Shift just ended. Followed up on the equipment ticket I submitted around 11:00 — the crash cart in Bay 3 still hadn't been restocked by the time we cleared the last critical at 18:40. Phil flagged it too. Want to make sure that gets logged properly before the overnight team comes in.

Also noticed the time stamp on my badge-in was off by about 20 minutes again. Third time this month. If the system is still glitching, payroll needs to know.

Home now. Coffee's already cold.

— Nate

Hi Dad,

Got both. Logging the Bay 3 crash cart issue now under Equipment/Critical Supplies and flagging it Priority 1 so facilities sees it before the overnight handoff at 19:30. They've been notified.

On the badge time-stamp: pulling your entry logs now. Three occurrences should auto-trigger a payroll correction request — I'll make sure it does and send you the case number once it's confirmed.

You sound done. Drink some water before you crash.

— Chloe

Chloe,

Got it. Case number when you have it.

Bay 3 — Dr. Engstrom can verify if they need a second contact. He was there for the back half of the shift.

Water. Yeah.

— N

Dad,

Case #HR-7741 for the badge issue — correction request is in, payroll has it. You'll see it reconciled on Friday's summary.

Bay 3 ticket is Priority 1, facilities confirmed restock before 20:00. Added Dr. Engstrom as secondary contact on the report.

Go rest. Mom already texted asking if you're home yet.

— Chloe
